title: Mardi Gras Parade — Bay St. Louis
content: Bay St. Louis celebrates Mardi Gras with a festive parade through Old Town and along Beach Boulevard. Local krewes, marching bands, and decorated floats fill the streets with beads, music, and celebration. The charming seaside setting makes this one of the most scenic Mardi Gras parades on the coast.
address: Main Street, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Mardi Gras Parade — Waveland
content: Waveland's Mardi Gras parade rolls through the city's streets with festive floats, marching bands, and plenty of beads. A beloved community tradition that brings out the whole town for a day of fun and celebration.
address: Coleman Avenue, Waveland, MS 39576

title: Second Saturday Art Walk — March
content: Explore Old Town Bay St. Louis and the Depot District during the monthly Second Saturday Art Walk! Discover cool deals, fresh meals, and live music as merchants and restaurants offer specials throughout the day. Galleries open their doors, artists demonstrate their craft, and the streets come alive with creativity.
venue: Old Town Bay St. Louis & Depot District
address: Main Street, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: SOUPER Mudfest 2026
content: Purchase a handcrafted soup bowl made by local artists for $25, then fill it with delicious soup from participating Bay St. Louis merchants starting at 2 PM. A beloved community tradition that combines local art with great food. Arrive early for the best selection of one-of-a-kind bowls!
address: 162 Main Street, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: St. Patrick's Day Parade — Waveland
content: Waveland celebrates St. Patrick's Day with a festive parade along the beachfront! The parade starts at Bienville Blvd and North Beach Blvd, rolling through Waveland with floats, marching bands, and plenty of green.
address: Bienville Blvd., Waveland, MS 39576

title: Arts Alive! BSL
content: Arts Alive is Bay St. Louis' premier two-day art festival in the Depot Arts District! This rain-or-shine event features multiple performance stages, an artist trail with award-winning local talent, interactive MakeSpace demonstrations, The Arts kids zone, delicious food, and live music. A celebration of the vibrant arts community that makes Bay St. Louis special.
address: 301 Blaize Avenue, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Hancock County Parade of Homes
content: Tour over 35 homes for sale across Hancock County during the annual Parade of Homes! Whether you're looking to buy or just love touring beautiful homes, this self-guided tour takes you through neighborhoods in Bay St. Louis, Waveland, Diamondhead, and beyond.
address: Hancock County, MS

title: Annual STrEaM'in The Coast
content: A fun day of hands-on Science, Technology, Recreation, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics activities for the whole family! Kids and adults alike can explore interactive STEAM exhibits, participate in workshops, and discover the exciting world of science and technology.
address: Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Inaugural Flower Festival
content: Bay St. Louis welcomes its Flower Festival — a two-day celebration of spring, flowers, creativity, and wellness! Enjoy blooming flower displays, inspiring art, food trucks, live music, and wellness activities. A beautiful way to welcome spring on the Gulf Coast.
address: Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Waveland Crawfish Cook-off
content: Teams compete for the Best Crawfish title and other awards at this beloved Waveland tradition! Held on Coleman Avenue, enjoy all-you-can-eat crawfish, live music, and a festive atmosphere. All proceeds benefit the Hancock Resource Center.
address: Coleman Avenue, Waveland, MS 39576

title: FAN*FEST 2026
content: The Hancock County Library System hosts FAN*FEST, a free fan convention for all ages! Celebrate your favorite fandoms with cosplay, panels, activities, and more. Whether you're into sci-fi, fantasy, gaming, anime, or comics, there's something for every fan.
address: Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Pirate Day in the Bay
content: Ahoy! The annual Pirate Day in the Bay takes over Bay St. Louis with a two-day pirate-themed festival. Friday features a pub crawl through Old Town. Saturday brings costume contests, a children's Lil' Buccaneer parade, a decorated golf cart parade, live music, and fireworks over the harbor. A swashbuckling good time for the whole family!
address: S. Beach Blvd. & Court St., Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Second Saturday Art Walk — May
content: The monthly Second Saturday Art Walk returns to Old Town Bay St. Louis and the Depot District. Galleries open their doors with new exhibits, restaurants offer specials, and live music fills the streets. A beloved tradition that showcases the artistic soul of Bay St. Louis.
venue: Old Town Bay St. Louis & Depot District
address: Main Street, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: St. Clare Seafood Festival
content: The annual St. Clare Seafood Festival returns to Waveland for Memorial Day weekend! This beloved multi-day celebration features fresh Gulf Coast seafood, a classic car show, live music, carnival rides, and craft vendors. All proceeds benefit St. Clare Catholic Church. A Waveland tradition not to be missed.
address: 236 S. Beach Blvd., Waveland, MS 39576

title: Diamondhead Art & Wine Festival
content: The Diamondhead Art & Wine Festival brings together local and regional artists, wine vendors, and live music in the beautiful Diamondhead community. Browse original artwork, sample wines from across the Southeast, and enjoy an elegant afternoon in a scenic setting.
address: 7600 Country Club Circle, Diamondhead, MS 39525

title: St. Rose de Lima Blues & BBQ Festival
content: The annual St. Rose de Lima Blues & BBQ Festival fills the L&N Historic Train Depot grounds with live blues, zydeco, and oldies music, smoky BBQ, seafood, and arts and crafts vendors. Hosted by St. Rose de Lima Catholic Church, this two-day festival is one of Bay St. Louis' most popular summer events. Free admission — food and drinks purchased separately.
address: 1928 Depot Way, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Juneteenth Celebration — Bay St. Louis
content: Bay St. Louis celebrates Juneteenth with a community event honoring African American history, culture, and freedom. The celebration features live music, spoken word performances, soul food, vendors, and family activities in the heart of downtown.
address: Main Street, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Second Saturday Art Walk — June
content: The monthly Second Saturday Art Walk continues in Old Town Bay St. Louis. Summer gallery openings, restaurant specials, live music, and the creative energy that makes BSL one of the top art communities in the South.
venue: Old Town Bay St. Louis & Depot District
address: Main Street, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Our Lady of the Gulf Crab Fest
content: One of Bay St. Louis' most beloved annual traditions! The Our Lady of the Gulf Crab Fest runs over the Fourth of July weekend with fresh Gulf Coast crab, seafood, live entertainment, and community celebration. Enjoy the festive atmosphere, delicious food, and fireworks over the bay. A true Hancock County tradition.
venue: Our Lady of the Gulf Catholic Church
address: 228 S. Beach Blvd., Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Fourth of July Celebration — Diamondhead
content: Diamondhead celebrates Independence Day with a community cookout, pool party, live music, kids' activities, and an evening fireworks display. A family-friendly celebration in one of Hancock County's premier planned communities.
address: Diamondhead, MS 39525

title: Bay St. Louis Regatta
content: Sailboats race across the Bay of St. Louis in this annual regatta hosted by the Bay-Waveland Yacht Club. Watch from the shore as colorful sails fill the bay, and enjoy waterfront festivities, food, and live music. A beautiful day on the water for sailors and spectators alike.
address: 100 Yacht Club Dr., Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Back to School Bash — Hancock County
content: Hancock County families prepare for the new school year at the annual Back to School Bash. Free school supplies, backpacks, haircuts, health screenings, and community resources. Fun activities and games keep the kids entertained.
address: Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Second Saturday Art Walk — August
content: Beat the summer heat with a stroll through Bay St. Louis' galleries and shops during the August Second Saturday Art Walk. New art exhibits, evening dining specials, live music, and the warm hospitality that makes this monthly tradition so popular.
venue: Old Town Bay St. Louis & Depot District
address: Main Street, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Kiln Heritage Festival
content: The Kiln Heritage Festival celebrates the rural traditions and community spirit of this charming Hancock County town. Enjoy craft demonstrations, bluegrass music, Southern cooking, tractor displays, and a look at the history and heritage of the Kiln area. Favre country at its finest!
address: Kiln, MS 39556

title: Booker Fest at 100 Men Hall
content: A three-day music festival celebrating piano legend James Booker, who was raised in Bay St. Louis. Held at the historic 100 Men Hall — a Mississippi Blues Trail site — Booker Fest features live performances from blues, R&B, and soul artists over Labor Day weekend. A fundraiser for the preservation of this iconic venue.
address: 303 Union St., Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Bay St. Louis Fall Art & Book Festival
content: A celebration of visual arts and literature in downtown Bay St. Louis. Browse original artwork, meet local and regional authors at book signings, attend readings and panel discussions, and enjoy the creative community that makes BSL a top arts destination.
address: 301 Blaize Avenue, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Boo in the Bay Parade
content: The annual Boo in the Bay Parade takes over Old Town Bay St. Louis with a family-friendly Halloween parade of decorated golf carts, Jeeps, and classic cars — all participants in costume! The parade rolls from Bookter Street through downtown to Main Street, followed by live music and local cuisine. Registration fills up quickly, so sign up early!
address: 128 Main Street, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Diamondhead Arts & Crafts Fair
content: The annual Diamondhead Arts & Crafts Fair features over 150 regional artists, crafts vendors, unique handmade gifts, and food trucks on the City Hall lawn. Running for over 36 years, this two-day fair is a Diamondhead tradition and a fundraiser for the Diamondhead Youth Athletic Association. Free admission.
address: 5500 Diamondhead Circle, Diamondhead, MS 39525

title: Veterans Day Ceremony — Bay St. Louis
content: Bay St. Louis honors all who have served with a Veterans Day ceremony at the Hancock County Courthouse. The event features patriotic music, guest speakers, a rifle salute, and wreath-laying. Open to the public.
address: 152 Main Street, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Taste of Hancock County
content: Sample signature dishes from 25+ local restaurants at the annual Taste of Hancock County! This culinary event showcases the best of Hancock County's dining scene with live music and activities. All proceeds support the Hancock County Tourism Bureau. Sponsored by the cities of Bay St. Louis, Waveland, and Diamondhead.
address: 301 Blaize Ave., Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Diamondhead BBQ & Brews Festival
content: An afternoon celebrating veterans with live music, food vendors, a BBQ competition, and a beer garden on the Diamondhead Town Green. Enjoy smoked meats, craft brews, and community spirit in a beautiful setting.
address: 5500 Diamondhead Circle, Diamondhead, MS 39525

title: Thanksgiving Turkey Trot 5K — Bay St. Louis
content: Start your Thanksgiving morning with the annual Turkey Trot 5K through scenic Bay St. Louis. Run or walk along the beachfront and through charming Old Town before the big feast. Costumes encouraged! Proceeds benefit local charities.
address: North Beach Blvd., Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Bay St. Louis Christmas Parade
content: Bay St. Louis kicks off the holiday season with its beloved Christmas parade through Old Town. Decorated floats, marching bands, dance teams, and Santa Claus make their way through the charming streets. After the parade, enjoy holiday shopping and dining in the unique shops and restaurants of downtown BSL.
address: Main Street, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Waveland Christmas Parade
content: Waveland celebrates the holidays with its annual Christmas parade through the city streets. Festive floats, community groups, school bands, and Santa spread holiday cheer throughout the community.
address: Coleman Avenue, Waveland, MS 39576

title: Christmas in the Bay
content: Bay St. Louis transforms into a holiday wonderland for Christmas in the Bay! Old Town shops stay open late with holiday specials, carolers stroll the streets, and a tree lighting ceremony caps off the evening. Enjoy hot cocoa, holiday music, and the warmth of this coastal community at Christmastime.
address: Main Street, Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Bay-Waveland Yacht Club Christmas Bazaar
content: The annual Bay-Waveland Yacht Club Christmas Bazaar features 30+ vendors offering art, crafts, jewelry, pottery, boutique items, and gourmet foods. Shop for unique holiday gifts with a view overlooking the bay. Lunch available. Hosted by the Ladies Auxiliary as a fundraiser. Free admission.
address: 100 Yacht Club Dr., Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Diamondhead Christmas Parade
content: The Diamondhead community celebrates the holiday season with a Christmas parade through the neighborhood streets. Golf carts and floats decked out in holiday lights and decorations make this a unique and charming community tradition.
address: Diamondhead, MS 39525

title: New Year's Eve Oyster Drop
content: Bay St. Louis rings in the New Year with its nationally famous Oyster Drop! A giant 7-foot oyster descends from atop 200 North Beach restaurant at midnight, accompanied by fireworks, a bonfire, live bands, and streamers. Featured by ABC News, this unique Gulf Coast tradition draws approximately 8,000 revelers to the beachfront. Free and open to the public.
address: 200 North Beach Blvd., Bay St. Louis, MS 39520

title: Pearlington Community Day
content: The Pearlington community comes together for an annual celebration featuring live music, home-cooked food, kids' games, and fellowship along the Pearl River. This tight-knit community honors its heritage and resilience with a day of togetherness.
address: Pearlington, MS 39572
